briefs.title: Burnaby updates how bylaw notices are enforced — rules now amended for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed an amendment to its Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw, updating the rules around how the city issues and enforces bylaw notices (official warnings or fines for breaking local rules).
briefs.action_required: Check if your business has any outstanding Burnaby bylaw notices, as enforcement rules have changed.

briefs.title: 190 Residents Petition Against Rezoning at 5058 Imperial Street — Council Informed
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council received a petition signed by 190 residents opposing rezoning application REZ #25-13 at 5058 Imperial Street. No decision has been made yet; the petition was noted for information only.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council advances zoning change at 5058 Imperial Street — development one step closer
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed the first and second readings of a zoning bylaw amendment for 5058 Imperial Street, moving a rezoning application (a request to change how land can be used) forward. Four council members voted against it.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease property near 5058 Imperial St, monitor future council readings and public hearings for this rezoning.

briefs.title: Burnaby updates parking meter and EV charging meter rules — what businesses need to know
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed an amendment to its Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw, updating the rules that govern paid parking and EV charging stations in the city.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business operates parking or EV charging meters in Burnaby and review the updated bylaw for compliance.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Burnaby updates its consolidated fees and charges bylaw — costs may change
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Amendment No. 2 to its Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw for 2026, meaning some municipal fees and charges in Burnaby may now be different.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated fee schedule to see if any fees you pay to the City have changed.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby rezones 6800 Lougheed Highway — first and second reading passed
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the first and second reading of a zoning bylaw amendment for 6800 Lougheed Highway, moving the rezoning application REZ #25-18 closer to approval. The property's permitted land uses may change once the bylaw receives final approval.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates at or near 6800 Lougheed Hwy, monitor future council readings for final approval.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby Rezoning at 2209 & 2225 Springer Ave Advances to First and Second Reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the first and second readings of a zoning bylaw amendment for two properties on Springer Avenue, moving the rezoning application one step closer to approval.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 2209–2225 Springer Ave, monitor City of Burnaby notices for a public hearing date.

briefs.title: Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan Implementation Action Plan project initiated
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has received the project initiation overview for the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP) Implementation Action Plan — a long-term blueprint guiding how the city will grow and develop over the coming decades.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Five-storey self-storage facility approved for 2757 Norland Avenue — rezoning bylaw in progress
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has approved moving forward with a rezoning bylaw to allow a five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue. No public hearing is needed because the project aligns with the city's Official Community Plan (the long-term land-use blueprint for the area).
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves Poverty Reduction Strategy; provincial funding advocacy underway
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved a Poverty Reduction Strategy and directed staff to build an action plan with community partners. The Mayor will also write to the province asking for poverty reduction funding.

briefs.title: City reviews 2025 investment results and sets 2026 financial forecast
briefs.summary: City council received the 2025 year-end investment report and 2026 forecast from its Financial Management Committee. No policy changes were made — council accepted the reports for information only.

briefs.title: 3900 Grand Promenade development expanded: office space replaced by hotel and 362 new strata units added
briefs.summary: Council approved changes to a mixed-use development at 3900 Grand Promenade, swapping planned office space for a hotel and restaurant, and adding 362 strata (privately owned condo) units. Affordable housing requirements tied to this site are being moved to a nearby location called the Carrigan site.
briefs.action_required: If you own or lease space near 3900 Grand Promenade, review how the added density and hotel use may affect your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service

briefs.title: 12-storey, 290-unit mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway moves closer to approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw for 6535 Kingsway, allowing a 12-storey building with 290 homes and ground-floor commercial space. The rezoning (a change to land-use rules) is not yet final but is now one step closer to passing.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby Council agendas for the public hearing date on this rezoning bylaw.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Municipal asphalt paving contract worth $1.155M renewed with Blackrete Paving
briefs.summary: A local council has renewed its asphalt paving services contract with Blackrete Paving Ltd. for $1,155,000 including taxes. This is a government procurement decision, not a new regulation.

briefs.title: Carrigan Court mixed-use development rezoning application approved to proceed
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has authorized staff to accept a rezoning application for 3856 & 3870 Carrigan Court, allowing a high-density mixed-use building with commercial and residential units to move forward.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the rezoning process if your business operates near or depends on this Carrigan Court site.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Modular Four-Plex Housing Pilot Contract Sent Back for Efficiency Review
briefs.summary: City council has paused the contract award for a modular four-plex housing pilot project at 8315 14th Avenue, sending it back to staff to find ways to manage the project more efficiently.

briefs.title: 19 contractors awarded hired equipment services contracts paid by actual quantities delivered
briefs.summary: Council approved contracts with 19 separate contractors to provide hired equipment services. Payments will be based on the actual amount of work or materials delivered, not a fixed price.

briefs.title: BC municipality approves $36,250.57 in UBCM membership dues for 2026
briefs.summary: Council has approved paying $36,250.57 (including taxes) in 2026 membership dues to the Union of BC Municipalities, the provincial association that represents local governments.

briefs.title: $4.2M water metering contract awarded to Neptune Technology Group Canada
briefs.summary: A municipal council awarded a $4.2 million contract to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. to supply and deliver water metering technology, taxes included.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority approves 2026–2030 Strategic Plan, shaping city housing development
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the Burnaby Housing Authority's five-year strategic plan for 2026–2030, setting the direction for affordable housing development in the city over the next five years.
briefs.action_required: Monitor BHA project announcements for development opportunities, zoning changes, or housing requirements affecting your property.
